program.title: Namma Kannada
program.subtitle: Learn Bangalore Kannada the way it is actually spoken

stages.name: Grammar Shapes
stages.description: Before learning vocabulary, learn how Kannada sentences are built. Word order is reversed from English. Yes and no work differently. Every pattern here will carry you through the next three stages.

sentences.gloss: Want coffee
sentences.grammar_note: beku ends the sentence. Always. Kannada is verb-final — the action comes last.

sentences.gloss: Don't want coffee
sentences.grammar_note: beku and beda are a pair. beku = yes I want. beda = no I don't.

sentences.gloss: Coffee is there
sentences.grammar_note: ide means something exists. Not 'is' as in identity — as in presence. Coffee ide = coffee is available.

sentences.gloss: Coffee is not there
sentences.grammar_note: ide and illa are the most useful pair in Kannada. Learn them as one unit.

sentences.gloss: Yes
sentences.grammar_note: haudu is a full sentence. You don't need anything else to say yes.

sentences.gloss: Ok / alright
sentences.grammar_note: sari is the most common word in Bangalore Kannada. Agreement, acknowledgement, moving on — all covered.

sentences.gloss: Done / ok it happened
sentences.grammar_note: aaytu closes things. Meeting over: aaytu. Food ordered: aaytu. Understood: aaytu.

sentences.gloss: Right? / isn't it?
sentences.grammar_note: Stick alvaa at the end of anything to make it a tag question. Like 'innit' in British English.

sentences.gloss: I want coffee
sentences.grammar_note: Subject goes first. Then object. Then verb. Naanu coffee beku = I coffee want.

sentences.gloss: Do you want coffee?
sentences.grammar_note: Rising intonation makes it a question. neevu is polite you — use with strangers, elders, anyone you'd call sir or ma'am.

sentences.gloss: What is that?
sentences.grammar_note: enu is what. adu is that. Adu enu = that what = what is that. Question word comes at the end.

sentences.gloss: What is this?
sentences.grammar_note: idu = this. adu = that. idu enu? = what is this?

sentences.gloss: Where is it?
sentences.grammar_note: elli = where. Combine with ide or illa: elli ide? = where is it? elli illa? = where is it not? (it's gone)

sentences.gloss: How much?
sentences.grammar_note: eshtu alone is a complete question. Price, quantity, distance, time — it covers all of them.

sentences.gloss: How much is coffee?
sentences.grammar_note: No verb needed for a price question. Coffee eshtu? = Coffee how much?

sentences.gloss: I don't know
sentences.grammar_note: gottilla is your most useful escape hatch. Also: gottu = I know / understood.

sentences.gloss: Where is the office?
sentences.grammar_note: Swap any noun: mane elli ide? = where is home? darshini elli ide? = where is the restaurant?

sentences.gloss: I am going to office
sentences.grammar_note: -ge attaches to the destination. mane-ge = to home. market-ge = to market. No space before it.

sentences.gloss: Come home
sentences.grammar_note: banni is come (polite). baa is come (casual/intimate). Use banni with anyone you don't know well.

sentences.gloss: What is there at the office?
sentences.grammar_note: -alli means in or at. mane-alli = at home. bus-alli = in the bus. -ge is movement, -alli is location.

sentences.gloss: There is a lot of traffic
sentences.grammar_note: tumbaa is your intensity word. tumbaa ide = a lot is there. tumbaa beda = way too much, don't want.

sentences.gloss: Need a little
sentences.new_word_meaning: a little / slightly / just a bit
sentences.grammar_note: svalpa and tumbaa are your quantity pair. svalpa beku = just a little. tumbaa beda = not so much.

sentences.gloss: There is a meeting today
sentences.grammar_note: Time words go first, before the subject. Ivattu naale eega — today tomorrow now — always at the front.

sentences.gloss: I will come tomorrow
sentences.grammar_note: barteeni = I will come / I am coming. The -teeni ending means I (first person, future/present continuous).

sentences.gloss: What are you doing now?
sentences.grammar_note: maadteeri = you are doing (polite). maadteeni = I am doing. Swap -teeni (I) / -teeri (you polite).

sentences.gloss: That is not right
sentences.grammar_note: alla is emphatic not. illa = does not exist. alla = is not the case. adu sari alla = that is not correct.

sentences.gloss: But what to do?
sentences.grammar_note: aadre is the pivot word. Adu sari alla — aadre enu maadodu? = That is not right — but what to do?

sentences.gloss: Need to make a call
sentences.grammar_note: English nouns slot straight in. phone-ge call beku = phone-to call want. The Kannada grammar holds, English words fill the slots.

sentences.gloss: Right, it has been a long time
sentences.grammar_note: tumbaa aaytu = a lot has happened / it's been a while. Literally: a lot has become.

sentences.gloss: Ok, let us talk tomorrow
sentences.grammar_note: The -ona ending means let us. hogona = let us go. maataadona = let us talk. A gentle way to close a conversation.

stages.name: Daily Survival
stages.description: The language of Bangalore daily life — commute, food, office, phone. Most nouns are still English but the grammar and verbs are Kannada. This is how newcomers to the city actually speak.

sentences.gloss: I am going to office
sentences.grammar_note: -teeni is the first-person present-continuous ending. hogteeni = I am going. barteeni = I am coming. maadteeni = I am doing.

sentences.gloss: Where are you going?
sentences.grammar_note: -teeri is the polite second-person ending. hogteeni = I am going. hogteeri = you are going (polite). Swap the ending to change the person.

sentences.gloss: I am coming home
sentences.grammar_note: baa = come (command). barteeni = I am coming. barteeri = you are coming. The verb root bar- carries the meaning, the ending carries the person.

sentences.gloss: When are you coming?
sentences.grammar_note: yaavaaga is when. It goes at the start of the question. yaavaaga barteeri = when are you coming? yaavaaga hogteeri = when are you going?

sentences.gloss: I am going by bus
sentences.grammar_note: -alli on a vehicle means by that vehicle. bus-alli = by bus. metro-alli = by metro. auto-alli = by auto. Same postposition, different meaning from location.

sentences.gloss: Let us go by metro
sentences.grammar_note: -ona is the let-us ending. hogona = let us go. kudiyona = let us drink. maataadona = let us talk. Works on any verb.

sentences.gloss: Auto will be available
sentences.new_word_meaning: will get / will be found / will be available
sentences.grammar_note: sigatte = will be found / available. auto sigatte = auto available. room sigatte = room available. signal sigatte = signal will come.

sentences.gloss: No auto available
sentences.new_word_meaning: will not be found / not available
sentences.grammar_note: sigatte and sigalla are the availability pair. sigatte = will find. sigalla = won't find. Works for anything you are looking for.

sentences.gloss: Traffic is a lot, will be late
sentences.grammar_note: aagatte = it will happen. late aagatte = will be late. ready aagatte = will be ready. It is the general future tense word.

sentences.gloss: There is a little time, come quickly
sentences.grammar_note: bega = quickly or soon. bega banni = come quickly. bega hogteeni = going fast. bega aagatte = will happen soon.

sentences.gloss: Is food done? / Have you eaten?
sentences.grammar_note: aaytu = done. oota aaytu = food is done / eating is done. Adding -aa at the end makes it a question. Short and completely natural.

sentences.gloss: I am eating
sentences.grammar_note: maadu = do / make. maadteeni = I am doing. oota maadteeni = I am eating (literally: doing food). kelasa maadteeni = I am working.

sentences.gloss: Need two idlis
sentences.grammar_note: In food orders, number comes after the item. idli eradu = two idlis. coffee ondu = one coffee. dosa mooru = three dosas. Then beku at the end.

sentences.gloss: Make it less spicy
sentences.grammar_note: maadi is the polite command form of maadu. kammi maadi = make it less. jaasti maadi = make it more. Any adjective + maadi = a food customization request.

sentences.gloss: Need very hot coffee
sentences.grammar_note: Repeating an adjective intensifies it. bisi = hot. bisi bisi = very hot. tumbaa tumbaa = extremely a lot. This doubling pattern is natural in Kannada.

sentences.gloss: How much did the bill come to?
sentences.grammar_note: eshtu aaytu = how much has it become. Works for price, time, quantity. eshtu aaytu = how much total. eshtu gamte aaytu = how many hours has it been.

sentences.gloss: Need some water
sentences.grammar_note: neeru is one of the first words you need anywhere. svalpa neeru beku = need a little water. cold water beku = need cold water. Direct and sufficient.

sentences.gloss: Where is the darshini?
sentences.grammar_note: darshini is the Bangalore word for a simple, clean vegetarian restaurant. Not hotel (which means any restaurant). Asking for a darshini signals you know the city.

sentences.gloss: Is the coffee ready?
sentences.grammar_note: English adjective + aaytu works naturally. ready aaytu = is ready. full aaytu = it is full. late aaytu = it got late. Aaytu is a workhorse.

sentences.gloss: The food was good
sentences.grammar_note: chennaagide = is good (present). chennaagittu = was good (past). Swap -ide for -ittu to shift to past tense. Works on most descriptors.

sentences.gloss: Where is the meeting?
sentences.grammar_note: elli ide is your location question template. Replace any noun: auto elli ide? wifi elli ide? washroom elli ide? Always works.

sentences.gloss: When is the meeting?
sentences.grammar_note: Same template, swap elli for yaavaaga. elli = where. yaavaaga = when. elli ide = where is it. yaavaaga ide = when is it.

sentences.gloss: I am at the office
sentences.new_word_meaning: I am (at a place) / I am here
sentences.grammar_note: iddini = I am here / I am present. Different from ide (it exists). iddini = I exist here. aaphees-alli iddini = I am at the office. mane-alli iddini = I am at home.

sentences.gloss: I will call / I am calling
sentences.grammar_note: English noun + maadteeni = doing that thing. call maadteeni = calling. work maadteeni = working. note maadteeni = taking notes. Highly productive pattern.

sentences.gloss: Can I make a call? / Is it ok to call?
sentences.new_word_meaning: can do / it is possible to do
sentences.grammar_note: maadabohudu = can be done / is permissible. hogubohuduaa? = can I go? koorabohuduaa? = can I sit? Adding -aa makes it a polite request.

sentences.gloss: Let us go for lunch
sentences.grammar_note: Any destination + -ge hogona = let us go to that place. market-ge hogona. mane-ge hogona. coffee-ge hogona (let us go for coffee).

sentences.gloss: Meeting is done, I am going home
sentences.grammar_note: Two clauses joined by a pause, no conjunction needed. X aaytu, Y hogteeni = X is done, going to Y. This is how Kannadigas structure multi-part sentences in casual speech.

sentences.gloss: Deadline is tomorrow
sentences.grammar_note: Time word + ide is the simplest way to state a schedule. meeting naale ide. holiday ivattu ide. delivery aamele ide.

sentences.gloss: I am working from home
sentences.grammar_note: Any English abbreviation slots into maadteeni. WFH maadteeni. OT maadteeni (doing overtime). PR maadteeni (raising a PR).

sentences.gloss: I am a little busy
sentences.grammar_note: English adjective + iddini = I am that way. busy iddini. tired iddini. free iddini (I am free / available). tumbaa busy iddini = I am very busy.

sentences.gloss: What time is it now?
sentences.grammar_note: eshtu gamte = how many hours = what time. eega eshtu gamte = what time is it now? mooru gamte aaytu = it has become 3 o'clock.

sentences.gloss: Will take ten minutes
sentences.grammar_note: number + nimisha aagatte = will take that many minutes. eshtu nimisha aagatte? = how many minutes will it take? eradu gamte aagatte = will take two hours.

sentences.gloss: When will you come in the morning?
sentences.grammar_note: beligge = morning. samje = evening. raatri = night. ivattu beligge = this morning. naale samje = tomorrow evening.

sentences.gloss: There is a meeting in the evening
sentences.grammar_note: Time of day goes at the front of the sentence. samje meeting ide. beligge coffee ide. raatri oota ide. Same pattern as ivattu and naale.

sentences.gloss: Let us talk after / later
sentences.grammar_note: aamele = after / later. aamele maataadona = let us talk later. aamele hogona = let us go later. Very useful to close a conversation politely.

sentences.gloss: Today got late
sentences.grammar_note: aaytu with an adjective means it became that. late aaytu = got late. full aaytu = got full. dark aaytu = got dark. It is the became word.

sentences.gloss: Do you have time?
sentences.grammar_note: hatra means near or with, but nimma hatra ide = you have it (literally: near you it exists). nanna hatra illa = I don't have it. The possession idiom in Kannada.

sentences.gloss: It is lunch time now
sentences.grammar_note: No verb needed for statements of fact in present context. eega lunch time. naale holiday. ivattu Friday. The noun is sufficient.

sentences.gloss: Come tomorrow morning
sentences.grammar_note: naale beligge = tomorrow morning. Stack time expressions freely. naale samje = tomorrow evening. ninne raatri = last night.

sentences.gloss: Wait a moment, I am coming
sentences.grammar_note: iri = stay / wait (polite command). svalpa iri = wait a bit. iru = the base verb. iddini = I am here. iri = you stay (polite).

sentences.gloss: Where will I find an auto?
sentences.grammar_note: elli sigatte = where will it be found. Works for anything you are searching for. wifi elli sigatte? good coffee elli sigatte? auto elli sigatte?

sentences.gloss: From the signal, go straight
sentences.grammar_note: -inda means from or by. signal-inda straight = from the signal, straight. mane-inda office = from home to office. bus-inda = by bus.

sentences.gloss: Take a right there
sentences.grammar_note: tago = take (casual). tagoli = take (polite). alli right tagoli = take a right there. alli left tagoli = take a left there.

sentences.gloss: How far is Indiranagar?
sentences.grammar_note: eshtu doora = how far. doora ide = it is far. hatra ide = it is near / close. tumbaa doora alla = not very far.

sentences.gloss: Can I park there?
sentences.grammar_note: maadabohudu = can do / is allowed. park maadabohuduaa? = can I park? U-turn maadabohuduaa? = can I U-turn? Any English action word + maadabohuduaa works.

sentences.gloss: I don't have cash
sentences.grammar_note: nanna = my. nimma = your (polite). avara = their/his/her (polite). nanna hatra = with me. nimma hatra = with you.

sentences.gloss: Does UPI work? / Will you take UPI?
sentences.grammar_note: aagattaa = will it work / is it ok. Card aagattaa? = does card work? Cash beda, UPI aagattaa? = no cash, is UPI ok? The universal payment question.

sentences.gloss: I will pay from phone
sentences.grammar_note: -inda with phone or app means via or using. phone-inda = via phone. app-inda = via app. Same -inda as from, extended to mean by means of.

sentences.gloss: Need a receipt
sentences.grammar_note: Any noun + beku is a complete, polite request. receipt beku. bill beku. change beku. bag beku. No extra words needed.

sentences.gloss: Is there any discount?
sentences.grammar_note: svalpa softens any request. svalpa kammi maadi = make it a little less. svalpa discount iddyaa = is there any discount. svalpa iri = wait just a bit.

sentences.gloss: How much is this?
sentences.grammar_note: The shortest price question. idu = this. eshtu = how much. Two words, complete sentence. Works anywhere — market, menu, cab fare.

sentences.gloss: It will be a hundred rupees
sentences.grammar_note: nooru = 100. nooru roopaayi = 100 rupees. aagatte = it will become (the price). eshtu roopaayi aagatte? = how many rupees will it be?

sentences.gloss: It has become too much
sentences.grammar_note: jaasti = more / excess. kammi = less. jaasti aaytu = it became too much. jaasti beda = don't want so much. kammi kodu = give less.

sentences.gloss: Reduce it please
sentences.grammar_note: kammi maadi = make it less. Works for price, spice, quantity, volume. plees at the end softens it further. Very natural bargaining phrase.

sentences.gloss: Do you have change?
sentences.grammar_note: iddyaa = is there / do you have (question form of ide). change iddyaa = is there change. time iddyaa = is there time. stock iddyaa = is it in stock.

sentences.gloss: Is this fresh?
sentences.grammar_note: English adjective + aa makes a yes-no question. fresh aa? = is it fresh? good quality aa? = is it good quality? original aa? = is it original?

sentences.gloss: Is there another color?
sentences.grammar_note: bere = other or different. bere color = another color. bere size = another size. bere option iddyaa = is there another option. Highly useful word.

sentences.gloss: I don't want this
sentences.grammar_note: nange = to me / for me. nanage is the full form, nange is the spoken shortening. nange beku = I want / need. nange beda = I don't want.

sentences.gloss: I will take that one
sentences.new_word_meaning: I am taking / I will take
sentences.grammar_note: tagolteeni = I am taking. idu tagolteeni = I'll take this. adu beda, idu tagolteeni = don't want that, taking this.

sentences.gloss: Give a bag please
sentences.grammar_note: kodi = give (polite). kodu = give (neutral). nimage kodu = giving to you. bag kodi = give a bag. receipt kodi = give a receipt.

sentences.gloss: How are you, are you doing well?
sentences.new_word_meaning: are you well / are you good (polite)
sentences.grammar_note: chennaagiddeera = are you fine (polite). chennaagiddini = I am fine. The complete greeting exchange in Bangalore Kannada.

sentences.gloss: It has been a long time
sentences.grammar_note: dina = day. tumbaa dina aaytu = many days have become = it has been a long time. eshtu dina aaytu = how many days has it been.

sentences.gloss: What is the scene? / What is going on?
sentences.grammar_note: scene is pure Bangalore slang. scene enu = what's happening. scene illa = not happening / no go. good scene ide = situation is good. Used constantly.

sentences.gloss: I am totally busy
sentences.grammar_note: full is a Bangalore intensifier. full busy = totally busy. full traffic ide = completely gridlocked. full tired aagiddini = I am completely tired. Different from English full.

sentences.gloss: Let us drink coffee
sentences.grammar_note: kudi = drink. kudiyona = let us drink. oota maadona = let us eat. samje coffee kudiyona = let us have coffee in the evening.

sentences.gloss: Let us meet tomorrow
sentences.new_word_meaning: let us meet / let us find each other
sentences.grammar_note: sigu = meet / find. sigona = let us meet. naale sigona = see you tomorrow. office-alli sigona = let us meet at the office.

sentences.gloss: That was great / brilliant
sentences.grammar_note: mast = awesome, from Hindi mast but fully absorbed into Bangalore Kannada. mast aaytu = that was great. mast ide = it is awesome. The most Bangalore compliment.

sentences.gloss: Don't worry / don't stress
sentences.grammar_note: maadkobeda = don't do it to yourself (casual). maadkobeedi = don't do it (polite). tension maadkobeda = don't stress. worry maadkobeda = don't worry.

sentences.gloss: Please accommodate / manage please
sentences.grammar_note: adjust maadkolli is the great Bangalore phrase. It asks someone to make do, be flexible, accommodate a situation. Used when things are not perfect but workable.

sentences.gloss: Ok, goodbye
sentences.grammar_note: namaskaara works for both hello and goodbye. sari, namaskaara = ok, goodbye. The most natural sign-off in Bangalore Kannada. aaytu, namaskaara = done, goodbye.

stages.name: Social Fluency
stages.tagline: Mostly Kannada, English where Kannada has no word
stages.description: Family, health, market, emotions. The conversations that matter beyond navigation. Bangalore-specific words start appearing here — mast, full, scene.

sentences.gloss: There are five people in our house
sentences.grammar_note: jana = person. aidu jana = five people. eshtu jana = how many people. iddaare = they are (polite third person plural). namma = our.

sentences.gloss: Father is at home now
sentences.grammar_note: appa = dad. amma = mom. iddaare = he/she is (polite, for elders and respected people). Never use ide for your own parents — use iddaare.

sentences.gloss: Mother is cooking
sentences.grammar_note: adige = cooking or kitchen. adige maadu = to cook (literally: do the cooking). amma adige maadteeri = mother is cooking (you would say to someone outside the family).

sentences.gloss: Elder brother is working in Bengaluru
sentences.grammar_note: anna = elder brother. akka = elder sister. tamma = younger brother. tangi = younger sister. iddaane = he is (casual, for peers). iddaare = he is (polite, for elders).

sentences.gloss: Elder sister's wedding is next month
sentences.grammar_note: maduve = wedding. tumbaa maduve scene ide Bengalooru-alli = there is a lot of wedding activity in Bangalore. mundina tingalu = next month. hecchina tingalu = previous month.

sentences.gloss: The child is going to school
sentences.grammar_note: magu = small child. makkalu = children. maga = son (older). magaLu = daughter. magu shaale-ge = child to school. eshtu vayassu? = how old?

sentences.gloss: I have come from our hometown
sentences.grammar_note: ooru = hometown or any town. namma ooru = our hometown. ooru-ninda = from hometown. -ninda is the from postposition (same as -inda but joins with n for flow).

sentences.gloss: Father has retired
sentences.grammar_note: English verb + aagiddaare = he/she has become that (polite, for elders). retire aagiddaare = has retired. transfer aagiddaare = has been transferred. promote aagiddaare = has been promoted.

sentences.kannada: ಅಮ್ಮ ಬೆಳಿಗ್ಗೆ ಬೇಗ ಎದ್ದು ಅಡಿಗೆ ಮಾಡ್ತಾರೆ
sentences.roman: amma beligge bega eddu adige maadtaare
sentences.gloss: Mother wakes up early in the morning and cooks
sentences.new_word_meaning: having woken up / getting up (verb connector)
sentences.grammar_note: eddu = having gotten up (connector form of eelu). Two actions connected: eddu adige maadu = wake up and cook. Verb + du/i connects sequential actions.

sentences.kannada: ನಾವು ರಾತ್ರಿ ಎಲ್ಲಾ ಒಟ್ಟಿಗೆ ಊಟ ಮಾಡ್ತೀವಿ
sentences.roman: naavu raatri ellaa ottige oota maadteevi
sentences.gloss: We all eat together at night
sentences.grammar_note: ottige = together. ellaa ottige = all together. naavu = we. maadteevi = we do (first person plural). The -teevi ending is we-present-continuous.

sentences.gloss: I have a fever
sentences.grammar_note: nanage X ide = I have X (literally: to me X exists). nanage jvara ide = I have fever. nanage novu ide = I have pain. The possession idiom applies to ailments too.

sentences.gloss: There is a headache
sentences.grammar_note: novu = pain. tale novu = headache (literally: head pain). hotte novu = stomach ache. kai novu = hand/arm pain. elli novu ide? = where is the pain?

sentences.gloss: Need to go to the doctor
sentences.grammar_note: hog + beku = need to go / must go. maad + beku = need to do. tinn + beku = need to eat. Verb root + beku = need to do that verb. One of the most useful constructions.

sentences.gloss: If you take medicine it will be fine
sentences.grammar_note: maatre = tablet or pill (specifically). ooshadhi = medicine in general. tagondre = if you take. verb + ndre = if you do that. hogondre aagatte = if you go it will work out.

sentences.gloss: The hospital is near from here
sentences.grammar_note: aspatre = hospital. illinda = from here. hatra ide = is near. illinda hatra ide = is near from here. doora illa = is not far (same meaning, different phrasing).

sentences.gloss: There is pain on this side, in the arm
sentences.grammar_note: kade = side or direction. ee kade = this side. aa kade = that side. kade is used for pointing at a body area or a direction. market kade = towards the market.

sentences.gloss: Fever has come down, I am fine now
sentences.grammar_note: ilidu = to come down / to reduce. jvara ilidide = fever has come down. rate ilidide = price has come down. tumbaa ilidide = it has come down a lot.

sentences.gloss: The doctor has said two days rest
sentences.grammar_note: helu = say/tell. heliddaare = has said (polite, for the doctor). heliddini = I have said. heliddira? = have you said?

sentences.gloss: My stomach is not right
sentences.grammar_note: hotte = stomach. hotte novu = stomach ache. hotte thumba ide = stomach is full. hotte sari illa = stomach is not right (polite way to say upset stomach).

sentences.gloss: Take good care of your health
sentences.grammar_note: aarogya = health. aarogya chennaagide = health is good. noodkolli = take care (literally: see to yourself). noodkobeku = need to take care. A caring send-off.

sentences.gloss: I am very happy / that made me very happy
sentences.grammar_note: khushi aaytu = became happy / felt happy. khushi ide = is happy. tumbaa khushi = very happy. From Hindi/Urdu but completely natural in Bangalore Kannada.

sentences.gloss: Hearing that made me sad
sentences.grammar_note: dukha aaytu = became sad. keli = having heard. adu keli = having heard that. Verb + i connects it to the result: heard + sad became.

sentences.gloss: I am feeling a lot of stress
sentences.grammar_note: nanage tension aagide = to me tension is happening. tension aagutte = tension will happen. tension maadkobeda = do not create tension for yourself.

sentences.gloss: He got very angry
sentences.grammar_note: kopa bantu = anger came = he got angry. kopa = anger. avnige = to him (casual). nanage kopa barutte = I will get angry. kopa illade iru = stay without anger = don't be angry.

sentences.gloss: Don't be afraid
sentences.grammar_note: bhaya = fear. bhaya padteeni = I am feeling afraid. padbeda = don't feel (casual). padbedi = don't feel (polite). bhaya padkobeedi = don't be afraid (to yourself).

sentences.gloss: Seeing this I was surprised
sentences.grammar_note: aashcharya aaytu = became surprised. idanna nodi = having seen this. -anna is the object marker: idanna = this (as an object). adanna = that. namanna = us.

sentences.gloss: It is genuinely very good
sentences.grammar_note: nijavaagi = truly. nijavaagluu = truly indeed (emphatic). Used when you want to convey sincerity and not just politeness. nijavaagloo mast ide = it is genuinely awesome.

sentences.gloss: Enough, no more
sentences.grammar_note: saaku = enough. saaku maadi = make it enough / stop. innu beda = no more. innu = more / still / yet. saaku, innu beda = enough, no more.

sentences.gloss: Give a little more
sentences.grammar_note: innoo = still more / a bit more. innoo beku = want more. innoo illa = still not there. innoo svalpa = a little more. A single word that covers still, yet, and more.

sentences.gloss: It is enough now / I have had enough
sentences.grammar_note: saakagide = has become enough. innu saakagide = now it is enough. Useful for politely declining food, work, a conversation that has run its course.

sentences.gloss: What does that mean? / What do you mean?
sentences.new_word_meaning: meaning / that is to say / so basically
sentences.grammar_note: amdre = meaning or that is to say. adu amdre enu? = what does that mean? amdre naanu hogbeku = meaning I need to go. The single most useful discourse word in Kannada.

sentences.gloss: So you mean you are not coming tomorrow?
sentences.grammar_note: anta = that (as a quote marker). baralla anta? = you are saying won't come? heliddaare anta = they said that. It closes reported speech.

sentences.gloss: Just not that / only that I don't want
sentences.grammar_note: maatra = only or just. adu maatra beda = only that I don't want (everything else is fine). naanu maatra hogteeni = only I am going (not others). Precise, emphatic.

sentences.gloss: Will anyone else come?
sentences.grammar_note: yaaru = who. yaaraadru = anyone / someone. enu + aadru = enaadru = something/anything. elli + aadru = ellaadru = somewhere. Adding aadru makes it open/indefinite.

sentences.gloss: This is different / This is another kind
sentences.grammar_note: tara = type or kind. bere tara = different kind. aa tara = that kind. hege tara = which kind. hege tara maadteeri? = how do you do it? (in what manner)

sentences.gloss: In that case I will go / if so I will go
sentences.new_word_meaning: in that case / if so / then
sentences.grammar_note: haagaadre = if that is so / in that case. haagirabeku = it should be like that. haage = like that / so. haagaadre is the pivot for conditional conclusions.

sentences.gloss: Even so it is ok, I will go
sentences.grammar_note: aadroo = even so / nevertheless. aadre = but/if. aadroo sari = even so it is fine. late aadroo sari = even if it is late, it is fine. Strong acceptance.

sentences.gloss: According to me it is not right
sentences.new_word_meaning: according to / in the opinion of
sentences.grammar_note: nanna prakaara = according to me / in my opinion. nimma prakaara = according to you. avara prakaara = according to them. A polite way to disagree.

sentences.gloss: That is exactly what I am saying
sentences.grammar_note: taane adds emphasis: ade taane = that very thing. naane = I myself. ninage taane = to you only. Used when you want to stress that something is exact.

sentences.gloss: What you said is right, isn't it
sentences.new_word_meaning: what was said / the thing that was said
sentences.grammar_note: verb + iddu = the thing that was done. helliddu = what was said. maadiddu = what was done. neevu helliddu = what you said. A nominalized verb, very common in Kannada.

sentences.gloss: The vegetables are fresh today
sentences.grammar_note: tarakaari = vegetables (general). tomato, eerulli (onion), aalu (potato) are common. fresh aagide = has become fresh = is fresh. ivattu fresh aagide = today it is fresh.

sentences.gloss: Give one kilo of tomatoes
sentences.grammar_note: keji = kilogram. eradu keji = two kilos. aadu keji = half kilo. tooka = weight. tookа maadi = weigh it. keji is the everyday word, not gram or kilo formally.

sentences.gloss: What is the price of onions?
sentences.grammar_note: eerulli = onion (specific Kannada word, used daily). ret = rate/price. eerulli ret jaasti aaytu = onion price has gone up. Common complaint in Bangalore markets.

sentences.gloss: Is this fresh?
sentences.grammar_note: taaja = fresh (from Hindi/Urdu, used for produce). fresh and taaja both work in Bangalore. taaja haalu = fresh milk. taaja tarkari = fresh vegetables.

sentences.gloss: Put the weight correctly
sentences.grammar_note: tooka = weight. tooka haaku = put/measure the weight. sariyaagi = correctly. tookа sari illa = the weight is not right. Useful when you suspect incorrect weighing.

sentences.gloss: Need one bunch of coriander
sentences.grammar_note: kattu = bunch or bundle. ondu kattu = one bunch. eradu kattu = two bunches. saasive kattu = bunch of mustard greens. kattu also means to tie or build.

sentences.gloss: Why is this so expensive?
sentences.grammar_note: tutti = expensive/costly. cheepu = cheap. tutti aaytu = it has become expensive. eshtu tutti = how expensive. ishtу tutti = this expensive (ishtu = this much).

sentences.gloss: Can I exchange this?
sentences.grammar_note: idna = idanna shortform = this (object). idna exchange maadabohuduaa = can this be exchanged. return maadabohuduaa = can it be returned. The -na object marker shortens in speech to -na.

sentences.gloss: The size is not right, give another
sentences.grammar_note: alate = size or measurement. alate sari illa = size is not right. alate nodi kodu = see the size and give. size and alate are both used in Bangalore shops.

sentences.gloss: Do you have small change?
sentences.grammar_note: chillare = small change or coins. change is also used but chillare is the Kannada word. chillare illa = no change. chillare kodu = give change.

sentences.gloss: What is your name?
sentences.grammar_note: hesaru = name. nimma hesrenu = what is your name (spoken shortening). nimma hesru enu? is the full form but hesrenu is how it is actually said. nanna hesru ___ = my name is ___.

sentences.gloss: Which place are you from?
sentences.new_word_meaning: person from a place / native of
sentences.grammar_note: ooru + inavaru = person from that place. yaava ooru-inavaru = from which place. Bengalooru-inavaru = person from Bengaluru. A friendly question to understand someone's background.

sentences.gloss: I know a little bit of Kannada
sentences.grammar_note: svalpa svalpa = little by little / a tiny bit (repetition softens). gottu = know. nanage kannada svalpa svalpa gottu = I know Kannada little by little. A humble and endearing way to say you are learning.

sentences.gloss: I am learning
sentences.new_word_meaning: I am in the process of learning
sentences.grammar_note: kali = learn. kaleetaa iddini = I am learning (ongoing process). maadtaa iddini = I am in the process of doing. The -taa iddini form signals work in progress.

sentences.gloss: I need your help
sentences.grammar_note: sahaaya = help. sahaaya beku = need help. sahaaya maadi = please help. nimma sahaaya beku = I need your help. A clear, dignified way to ask for help.

sentences.gloss: There was one thing / I had something (to ask)
sentences.new_word_meaning: was there / there was (past of ide)
sentences.grammar_note: ittu = was there (past tense of ide). ondu kelasa ittu = there was one task/thing. A common conversation opener meaning I have something to ask you.

sentences.gloss: I needed to talk to you
sentences.grammar_note: jote = with (for people). nimma jote = with you. avara jote = with them. hatra = near/with (for things). jote = with (for people and activities done together).

sentences.gloss: If you get time come once
sentences.grammar_note: omme = once / one time. eradu omme = twice. sikkidre = if you get/find. samaya sikkidre = if time is found / if you get time. A warm, low-pressure invitation.

sentences.gloss: Will you give your number?
sentences.grammar_note: kodteera? = will you give? kodu = give. kodteeni = I will give. kodteera = will you give. nimma number kodu = give your number (direct). kodteera = will you give (softer).

sentences.gloss: Let us meet again
sentences.grammar_note: matte = again. matte banni = come again. matte maadi = do it again. matte sigona = let us meet again. A warm goodbye that leaves the door open.

sentences.gloss: Have you eaten?
sentences.new_word_meaning: did you do / have you done (polite)
sentences.grammar_note: maadidra = did you do / have you done (polite question). maadidini = I did. maadiddaare = he/she has done. Asking if someone has eaten is a standard Kannada greeting.

sentences.gloss: There is food at home
sentences.grammar_note: adige = cooked food or cooking. mane-alli adige ide = there is food at home (meaning cooked food ready). hotel-ge hogbeda = no need to go to a restaurant.

sentences.gloss: There is rice, rasam, and stir-fry
sentences.grammar_note: anna = cooked rice. saaru = rasam (thin spiced soup). palya = dry vegetable stir-fry. The core Karnataka meal. sambaar is the thicker lentil-vegetable curry.

sentences.gloss: Shall I put ghee?
sentences.grammar_note: tuppa = ghee. haakla? = shall I put? (haaklaa = haaku + laa, the shall-I form). haakla is a warm hosting question. neeru haakla = shall I pour water?

sentences.gloss: It is enough, it has been a lot
sentences.new_word_meaning: it has become enough / I am full
sentences.grammar_note: saakaaytu = saaku + aaytu = it has become enough. Used to politely decline more food. Stronger than saaku alone. Always appreciated at a host's table.

sentences.gloss: Eat a little more
sentences.grammar_note: tinnu = eat (verb). tinni = eat (polite command). tinnbeedi = do not eat (polite). innu svalpa tinni = eat just a little more. The persistent hosting phrase.

sentences.gloss: Did you eat something sweet?
sentences.grammar_note: sihi = sweet (taste). khaara = spicy. huli = sour. uppu = salty. kaara = bitter. sihi tindi = sweet snack. sihi tindra = did you have sweets? (after a meal).

sentences.gloss: I cannot function without coffee
sentences.grammar_note: illade = without (literally: not being there). kaaphi illade = without coffee. neevu illade = without you. illade aagalla = cannot do without it. The dependency construction.

sentences.gloss: Will you give a glass of water?
sentences.grammar_note: lota = glass (for drinking, typically stainless steel). cup is also used. ondu lota neeru = one glass of water. eradu cup coffee = two cups of coffee.

sentences.gloss: The meal was good, thank you
sentences.grammar_note: dhanyavaada = thank you (formal, respectful). thanks is also common. swalpa tondare aaytu = caused you a little trouble (said when leaving). A gracious way to end a meal visit.

sentences.gloss: He is a very good person
sentences.grammar_note: manushya = person (more character-focused than jana which counts people). olle manushya = good person. manasa olle manushya = person with a good heart.

sentences.gloss: She is very clever / sharp
sentences.grammar_note: chalaaki = clever or sharp (from Hindi chalak). avalu chalaaki = she is smart. chalaaki maadu = to be clever about something. Often said with admiration.

sentences.gloss: They have been very helpful
sentences.grammar_note: English adjective + aagiddaare = he/she has been that way (sustained state, polite). helpful aagiddaare. supportive aagiddaare. strict aagiddaare. Very natural way to describe someone.

sentences.gloss: He speaks Kannada very well
sentences.new_word_meaning: it comes / he knows / he is capable of (for skills)
sentences.grammar_note: baratte = it comes to him = he knows how to. kannada baratte = he knows Kannada. khaana maadodu baratte = he knows how to cook. The skill possession idiom.

sentences.gloss: I do not know how to swim
sentences.grammar_note: eeju = swimming. eeju baralla = swimming does not come = I cannot swim. drive baratte = knows how to drive. baratte/baralla is the skill can/cannot pair.

sentences.gloss: Need to learn little by little
sentences.grammar_note: kali = to learn. kalibeku = need to learn. kaleetaiddini = I am learning. kalitini = I learned. svalpa svalpa kali = learn little by little. Encouragement for any learning journey.

sentences.gloss: I did not know that
sentences.grammar_note: gottu = know. gottilla = don't know (present). gottirallilla = did not know (past). The past negative adds -irallilla. Long but very common: gottirallilla sari aaytu = I didn't know, fair enough.

sentences.gloss: Now I understand / now I got it
sentences.grammar_note: gottaaytu = it became known = I understood. eega gottaaytu = now I get it. A natural response when something is explained to you. gottu + aaytu = understanding achieved.

sentences.gloss: Will you say it again?
sentences.grammar_note: matte + omme = mattomme = one more time. mattomme helu = say it again. mattomme maadu = do it again. Polite way to ask for repetition when you did not understand.

sentences.gloss: Please speak slowly
sentences.grammar_note: nidhaana = slow or careful. nidhaanavaagi = slowly. bega = fast. nidhaanavaagi maataadi = speak slowly please. nidhaanavaagi hogi = go slowly / drive carefully.

sentences.gloss: It looks like rain will come today
sentences.grammar_note: male = rain. male baratte = rain will come. male banthу = rain came. kaanatte = it looks like / it appears. anta kaanatte = it seems that. Weather small talk in Bangalore.

sentences.gloss: The rain has stopped
sentences.new_word_meaning: has stopped / has stood (past, present result)
sentences.grammar_note: nillu = to stop/stand. nintide = has stopped (with current relevance). male nintide = rain has stopped (so we can go now). bus nintide = bus has stopped.

sentences.gloss: The sun / heat is very intense
sentences.grammar_note: bisilu = sunlight or sunshine heat. bisi = hot (temperature). bisilu = the sun's heat specifically. bisilu tumbaa ide = it is very hot outside. nimala tagoli = take an umbrella.

sentences.gloss: It is very cold today
sentences.grammar_note: chali = cold (weather or feeling). bisilu = hot sun. male = rain. chali tumbaa ide = very cold. chali aagide = it has become cold. sweater haakoli = please wear a sweater.

sentences.gloss: The wind is blowing a lot
sentences.grammar_note: gaali = wind. gaali beesu = to blow (wind). beestide = is blowing. gaali tumbaa beestu ide = the wind is blowing very much. fresh gaali ide = fresh air is there.

sentences.gloss: Day after tomorrow is a holiday
sentences.grammar_note: ivattu = today. naale = tomorrow. naadiddu = day after tomorrow. ninne = yesterday. monnine = day before yesterday. raje = holiday or leave day.

sentences.gloss: There was a lot of work this week
sentences.grammar_note: ee vaara = this week. mundina vaara = next week. hinde vaara = last week. ee vaara tumbaa kelasa ittu = this week there was a lot of work.

sentences.gloss: Salary comes at the end of the month
sentences.grammar_note: sambala = salary. tingala konege = at month end. sambala baratte = salary will come. sambala aaytu = salary has come. The most anticipated sentence of the month.

sentences.gloss: Need to take a small loan
sentences.grammar_note: tagobbekide = need to take (tagо + beku + ide = taking is needed). Combines the need-to construction with ide for a softer, more tentative meaning. Useful for uncertain plans.

sentences.gloss: This month expenses have been a lot
sentences.grammar_note: kharchu = expense (from Urdu). kharchu jaasti aaytu = expenses have gone up. kharchu kammi maadbekuu = need to reduce expenses. ee tingalu kharchu tumbaa aaytu = this month spending was a lot.

sentences.gloss: Bengaluru has changed a lot
sentences.grammar_note: badalaaagu = to change. badalaagide = has changed. Bengalooru tumbaa badalaagide = Bengaluru has changed a lot. The standard opening for any conversation about the city's transformation.

sentences.gloss: It was not like this before
sentences.grammar_note: modalu = before or first. modalu heegittu = it used to be like this. modalu heegirallilla = it was not like this before. Often heard in conversations about Bangalore's rapid change.

sentences.gloss: Now there are a lot of people (population has grown)
sentences.grammar_note: jana aagiddaare = people have happened = population has grown. tumbaa jana aagiddaare = so many people have come. This is how Bangaloreans comment on population growth.

sentences.gloss: How many years have you been here?
sentences.grammar_note: varsha = year. eshtu varsha = how many years. varsha-dinda = since years. illiddiri = you have been here (polite). eshtu varsha-dinda Bengalooru-alli iddiri = how many years in Bangalore.

sentences.gloss: It has been ten years since coming
sentences.grammar_note: hattu varsha aaytu bandu = ten years have become since having come. bandu = having come (connector). The pattern: time + aaytu + having-done.

sentences.gloss: Now I am used to it / now it is habit
sentences.grammar_note: abhyaasa aagide = habit has formed = I am used to it. abhyaasa aagilla = not used to it yet. Bengalooru-ge abhyaasa aaytu = got used to Bangalore.

sentences.gloss: Which is your hometown?
sentences.grammar_note: yaavudu = which one. yaava = which (before a noun). nimma ooru yaavudu = your hometown which one is it. nimma ooru yaava = which is your town (before ooru).

sentences.gloss: I am from the Mysuru side
sentences.new_word_meaning: person from that side / from that direction
sentences.grammar_note: kade = direction/side. kadeyavanu = person from that side. Mysuru kadeyavanu = from the Mysore direction/region. A casual way to state your origin, less specific than naming the town.

sentences.gloss: Our Kannada is different in different places
sentences.grammar_note: bere bere = different different = varied. Mysuru kannada, Dharwad kannada, Mangalore kannada are quite distinct. Bengaluru kannada is a blend. This sentence comes up when comparing dialects.

sentences.gloss: But you can understand it right
sentences.grammar_note: artha = meaning or understanding. artha aagatte = meaning will come = you will understand. artha aagalla = cannot understand. adu amdre enu, artha aagalla = what does that mean, I cannot understand.

stages.name: Naturalistic Bangalore
stages.description: How fluent Bangalore speakers actually talk. Code-switching is intentional here, not a gap. You know when to use the English word for effect and when the Kannada word lands better.

sentences.kannada: ಹೌದು, Silk Board ಹತ್ರ ಅಲ್ವಾ? Daily ಅದೇ ಕಥೆ
sentences.roman: haudu, Silk Board hatra alvaa? daily ade kathe
sentences.gloss: Yes, near Silk Board right? Same story every day
sentences.grammar_note: ade kathe = same story = same old situation. daily ade kathe = every day the same story. Silk Board is Bangalore's most notorious traffic signal. Saying this bonds you instantly with any commuter.

sentences.kannada: Outer Ring Road-ನಲ್ಲಿ traffic ತುಂಬಾ bad ಆಗಿದೆ, ಒಂದು ಗಂಟೆ ತಡ ಆಯ್ತು
sentences.roman: Outer Ring Road-nalli traffic tumbaa bad aagide, ondu gamte tada aaytu
sentences.gloss: Traffic on Outer Ring Road is very bad, got late by one hour
sentences.grammar_note: tada = late or delay. tada aaytu = got late. eshtu tada aaytu = how late did it get. tumbaa tada aaytu = got very late. Late and tada are both used — late is more common in speech.

sentences.kannada: Metro-ಅಲ್ಲಿ ಹೋಗೋದೇ ವಾಸಿ, traffic-ಅಲ್ಲಿ ಸಿಕ್ಕಿ ಹಾಕ್ಕೊಳ್ಳೋದು ಬೇಡ
sentences.roman: metro-alli hogode vaasi, traffic-alli sikki haakkolodu beda
sentences.gloss: Better to go by metro, no point getting stuck in traffic
sentences.grammar_note: vaasi = better. metro-alli hogode vaasi = going by metro is better. aadru vaasi = even that is better. sikki haakkolodu = getting stuck (literally: catching and putting oneself).

sentences.kannada: ಇವತ್ತು traffic ತುಂಬಾ ಇತ್ತು, ಒಂದು ಗಂಟೆ late ಆಯ್ತು
sentences.roman: ivattu traffic tumbaa ittu, ondu gamte late aaytu
sentences.gloss: Today there was a lot of traffic, got late by one hour
sentences.grammar_note: ittu = was there (past of ide). traffic tumbaa ittu = there was a lot of traffic. The pattern: situation + ittu, consequence + aaytu. Two-clause commute complaint, extremely common.

sentences.kannada: Auto-ಅವನು meter ಹಾಕ್ತಾ ಇಲ್ಲ, extra ಕೇಳ್ತಾ ಇದ್ದಾನೆ
sentences.roman: auto-avanu meter haaktaa illa, extra keltaa iddaane
sentences.gloss: The auto driver is not running the meter, asking for extra
sentences.grammar_note: auto-avanu = the auto person / the auto guy. -avanu makes any noun into the person associated with it. bus-avanu = bus conductor. shop-avanu = shopkeeper. iddaane = he is (casual).

sentences.kannada: Namma Metro purple line-ಅಲ್ಲಿ ಹೋದ್ರೆ direct ಆಗತ್ತೆ
sentences.roman: Namma Metro purple line-alli hodre direct aagatte
sentences.gloss: If you go on Namma Metro purple line it will be direct
sentences.grammar_note: hogu + dre = hogondre → spoken as hodre = if you go. maadidre = if you do. nod-idre = if you see. Verb + (o)ndre / dre = if you do that verb.

sentences.kannada: ಅಲ್ಲಿ right ತಗೊಂಡು ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಹೋದ್ರೆ ಕಾಣ್ತದೆ
sentences.roman: alli right tagomdu svalpa hodre kaantade
sentences.gloss: Take a right there and go a little and you will see it
sentences.new_word_meaning: will be visible / will be seen
sentences.grammar_note: kaanu = to be visible / to see. kaantade = will be visible. Signal kaantade = the signal will be visible. illinda kaantade = visible from here. Directions in Bangalore almost always end with kaantade.

sentences.kannada: Maps ಮೇಲೆ ಒಂದು pin drop ಮಾಡಿ, ನಾನು ಬರ್ತೀನಿ
sentences.roman: Maps mele omdu pin drop maadi, naanu barteeni
sentences.gloss: Drop a pin on Maps, I will come
sentences.grammar_note: mele = on or above. Maps mele = on Maps. table mele = on the table. mele haaku = put on top. phone mele = on the phone. -alli is in, mele is on.

sentences.kannada: ಇಲ್ಲಿಂದ 5 minutes ಆದ್ರೆ Indiranagar metro station
sentences.roman: illinda 5 minutes aadre Indiranagar metro station
sentences.gloss: 5 minutes from here is Indiranagar metro station
sentences.grammar_note: illinda X aadre = X from here. illinda 5 minutes = 5 minutes from here. time + aadre + place = distance expression. No verb needed — the structure says it all.

sentences.kannada: Excuse me, MG Road-ಗೆ ಯಾವ line ತಗೋಬೇಕು?
sentences.roman: Excuse me, MG Road-ge yaava line tagobeku?
sentences.gloss: Excuse me, which line do I need to take for MG Road?
sentences.grammar_note: yaava line tagobeku = which line to take. tagobeku = need to take. A complete metro navigation question using only the patterns from Stage 2. Natural Bangalore English-Kannada blend.

sentences.kannada: ಅಣ್ಣ, ಒಂದು masala dose, ಒಂದು filter coffee
sentences.roman: anna, omdu masaale dose, omdu philtar kaaphi
sentences.gloss: Brother, one masala dosa, one filter coffee
sentences.grammar_note: Calling the server anna = brother is standard in Bangalore eateries. No beku needed — in a restaurant context the order is understood. anna and akka are respectful address for strangers.

sentences.kannada: ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಕಮ್ಮಿ ಮಾಡಿ ಅಣ್ಣ, ಅಲ್ಲಿ ಮೂವತ್ತಕ್ಕೆ ಕೊಡ್ತಾರೆ
sentences.roman: svalpa kammi maadi anna, alli moovattakke kodtaare
sentences.gloss: Make it a little less brother, they give for thirty over there
sentences.new_word_meaning: they give / they sell (third person plural)
sentences.grammar_note: kodtaare = they give. telling the seller that a competitor gives it cheaper. -takke = for (price marker). moovattakke = for thirty. noorutakke = for hundred. Classic Bangalore bargaining.

sentences.kannada: ಇದು fresh ಆ? ಇವತ್ತು ಬೆಳಿಗ್ಗೆ ಬಂದಿದ್ದಾ?
sentences.roman: idu fresh aa? ivattu beligge bandiddaa?
sentences.gloss: Is this fresh? Did it come this morning?
sentences.new_word_meaning: did it come / has it arrived
sentences.grammar_note: bandu + idda = bandidda = had come. bandiddaa? = did it come? (question). The double question is natural when inspecting produce at a market — confirm freshness then confirm delivery time.

sentences.kannada: ಹೌದು, ಎರಡೂ ಕೊಡಿ, ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಖಾರ ಕಮ್ಮಿ ಮಾಡಿ
sentences.roman: haudu, eradoo kodi, svalpa khaara kammi maadi
sentences.gloss: Yes, give both, make the spice a little less
sentences.grammar_note: eradu = two. eradoo = both (eradu + oo). Both the number and the both-meaning come from the same word. moostoo = all three. ellaaroo = everyone. The -oo suffix makes it inclusive/total.

sentences.kannada: ಈ food-ನ pack ಮಾಡಿ, ಮನೆಗೆ ತಗೊಂಡು ಹೋಗ್ತೀನಿ
sentences.roman: ee food-na pack maadi, manege tagomdu hogteeni
sentences.gloss: Pack this food, I will take it home
sentences.new_word_meaning: I will take and go / taking with me
sentences.grammar_note: tagomdu = having taken. hogteeni = I am going. tagomdu hogteeni = taking and going. Two verb connectors: do X and go = X + omdu + hogi. kelasa maadi hogteeni = doing work and going.

sentences.kannada: Pharmacy night 9 o'clock ವರೆಗೂ open ಇರತ್ತೆ
sentences.roman: pharmacy night 9 o'clock varegoo open iratte
sentences.gloss: The pharmacy will be open until 9 at night
sentences.grammar_note: varegu = until. varegoo = until (emphatic). raatri varegoo = until night. eega varegu = until now. eshtu varegoo = until how long. An important word for planning anything time-sensitive.

sentences.gloss: Should I take before food or after food?
sentences.grammar_note: modalu = before. namtara = after. food modalu = before food. food namtara = after food. The medicine timing question. modalu and namtara are your before-and-after pair.

sentences.kannada: ಇದು fit ಆಗ್ತಿಲ್ಲ ಅಂದ್ರೆ return ಮಾಡಬಹುದಾ?
sentences.roman: idu fit aagtilla amdre return maadabohudaa?
sentences.gloss: If this does not fit can I return it?
sentences.grammar_note: fit aagtilla amdre = if it doesn't fit. amdre introduces the condition. return maadabohuduaa = can I return it. A complete conditional question using Stage 1 and Stage 2 patterns together.

sentences.kannada: ಅದು ತುಂಬಾ expensive ಆಗಿದೆ, ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ cheaper ಏನಾದ್ರೂ ತೋರಿಸಿ
sentences.roman: adu tumbaa expensive aagide, svalpa cheaper enaadroo torisi
sentences.gloss: That is too expensive, show me something a little cheaper
sentences.grammar_note: torisu = to show. torisi = show (polite command). adu torisi = show that. options torisi = show the options. enaadroo torisi = show something (anything).

sentences.kannada: ನೋಡಿ, ಬೇರೆ ಕಡೆ ಇದಕ್ಕಿಂತ ಕಮ್ಮಿಗೆ ಸಿಗತ್ತೆ
sentences.roman: nodi, bere kade idakkinta kammige sigatte
sentences.gloss: Look, elsewhere this is available for less
sentences.grammar_note: idakkinta = than this. adakkinta = than that. idakkinta cheepu = cheaper than this. idakkinta olle = better than this. -kkinta is the comparison marker, attaches to the thing being compared against.

sentences.kannada: ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿದೆ! ಆದ್ರೆ ಕನ್ನಡ ಗೊತ್ತಿಲ್ಲ, ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಕಲೀತಿದ್ದೀನಿ
sentences.roman: tumbaa chennaagide! aadre kannada gottilla, svalpa svalpa kaleetiddeeni
sentences.gloss: It is very good! But I do not know Kannada, I am learning little by little
sentences.new_word_meaning: I am in the process of learning (ongoing)
sentences.grammar_note: kaleetiddeeni = I am learning (ongoing, emphasizes the process). kaleetaiddini is the same thing. -taa iddeeni/-tiddeeni signals an ongoing process you are engaged in. The natural learner's self-introduction.

sentences.kannada: ನಿಮ್ಮ ಕನ್ನಡ ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿದೆ, ಎಲ್ಲಿ ಕಲಿತ್ರಿ?
sentences.roman: nimma kannada tumbaa chennaagide, elli kalitri?
sentences.gloss: Your Kannada is very good, where did you learn?
sentences.grammar_note: kali + tiri = kalitiri → spoken as kalitri = where did you learn (polite). The warmest compliment a Kannadiga can give. Often follows the previous sentence pattern.

sentences.gloss: My Kannada had mistakes right?
sentences.grammar_note: tappu = mistake. tappu aaytu = a mistake happened. tappu maadteeni = I am making a mistake. tappu illa = no mistake. Said self-deprecatingly after attempting Kannada — usually met with warm reassurance.

sentences.gloss: If one more year passes I will be fluent
sentences.grammar_note: innu + ondu = innomdu = one more. innomdu varsha = one more year. innomdu cup = one more cup. innu svalpa = a little more. The aspirational learner sentence.

sentences.kannada: Kannada-ಅಲ್ಲಿ ಮಾತಾಡೋದು ಕಷ್ಟ ಆಗ್ತಿದೆ, ಆದ್ರೆ try ಮಾಡ್ತೀನಿ
sentences.roman: Kannada-alli maataadodu kashta aagide, aadre try maadteeni
sentences.gloss: Speaking in Kannada is becoming difficult, but I will try
sentences.grammar_note: kashta = difficult or hardship. kashta aagide = it is becoming difficult. kashta aagalla = it will not be difficult. aadre try maadteeni = but I will try. The honest learner's admission.

sentences.kannada: Halli Kannada aadre nange artha aagalla
sentences.roman: halli Kannada aadre nange artha aagalla
sentences.gloss: If it is village Kannada I cannot understand
sentences.grammar_note: halli = village. halli Kannada = village / rural Kannada. Bengalooru Kannada = city Kannada. halli-inda bandiddaare = came from village. Halli and city are natural conversation topics.

sentences.kannada: ನಿಮ್ಮ ಉಚ್ಚಾರ ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿದೆ, ಹೇಗೆ ಕಲಿತ್ರಿ?
sentences.roman: nimma uchchaara tumbaa chennaagide, hege kalitri?
sentences.gloss: Your pronunciation is very good, how did you learn?
sentences.grammar_note: uchchaara = pronunciation. uchchaara chennaagide = pronunciation is good. uchchaara sari illa = pronunciation is not right. hege kalitri = how did you learn (polite past).

sentences.kannada: ನಾನು ಅಲ್ಲೇ ಇದ್ದೆ, ಅದಕ್ಕೆ ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ ಗೊತ್ತಾಯ್ತು
sentences.roman: naanu alle idde, adakke svalpa gottaaytu
sentences.gloss: I was right there, so I understood a little
sentences.grammar_note: adakke = so / therefore. adu + ke = for that = so. tumbaa kelasa ittu, adakke hogalilla = there was a lot of work, so could not go. The causal connector in everyday speech.

sentences.kannada: Context ಇದ್ದ್ರೆ artha ಆಗತ್ತೆ, ಇಲ್ಲ ಅಂದ್ರೆ ಕಷ್ಟ
sentences.roman: context iddre artha aagatte, illa amdre kashta
sentences.gloss: If there is context it will make sense, otherwise it is difficult
sentences.grammar_note: iddre = if there is (ide + re). illa amdre = if not (illa + amdre). context iddre artha aagatte = if context is there meaning will come. illa amdre kashta = if not, difficult. Real Bangalore discourse about language.

sentences.gloss: Should have asked once, it would have been fine
sentences.grammar_note: saari = time/instance. omdu saari = once. eradu saari = twice. aa saari = that time / that instance. omdu saari kelu = ask once. The regret construction: bekittu = should have.

sentences.kannada: Meeting cancel ಆಯ್ತು, ಮನೆಯಿಂದಲೇ work ಮಾಡ್ತೀನಿ
sentences.roman: meeting cancel aaytu, maneyindalle work maadteeni
sentences.gloss: Meeting is cancelled, will work from home itself
sentences.new_word_meaning: from here itself / right from here (emphatic)
sentences.grammar_note: -inda + le = from here emphatic. maneyindalle = from home itself (not going anywhere). illi indalle = right from here. The -le suffix adds emphasis of staying in place.

sentences.kannada: ನಾನು ಇವತ್ತು WFH, ಮನೆಯಿಂದ join ಮಾಡ್ತೀನಿ
sentences.roman: naanu ivattu WFH, maneyinda join maadteeni
sentences.gloss: I am WFH today, will join from home
sentences.grammar_note: WFH as a word is fully absorbed. join maadteeni = I will join. Informing the team — this is the sentence structure every Bangalore tech worker uses.

sentences.gloss: I will send the link, please join
sentences.new_word_meaning: do it for yourself / please do (polite self-action)
sentences.grammar_note: maadkolli = please do it (you do it for yourself, politely). join maadkolli = please join. install maadkolli = please install. The polite self-directed command, very common for tech instructions.

sentences.gloss: You are on mute, cannot hear anything
sentences.new_word_meaning: cannot hear / is not being heard
sentences.grammar_note: kelu = hear/listen. keltilla = not hearing (present negative). enu keltilla = cannot hear anything. You are on mute said in Kannada — perhaps the most contemporary Bangalore sentence.

sentences.kannada: Deadline naale ide, aadre kеlasa inda aagilla
sentences.roman: deadline naale ide, aadre kelasa inda aagilla
sentences.gloss: Deadline is tomorrow but the work is not getting done
sentences.new_word_meaning: is not happening / is not getting done
sentences.grammar_note: inda aagilla = it is not happening from this side = it is not getting done. kelasa inda aagilla = work is not getting done. aagatte = will happen. aagilla = will not happen. The key distinction.

sentences.gloss: I will finish this within evening
sentences.grammar_note: olage = within or inside. evening-olage = within evening. ondu gamte-olage = within one hour. mane-olage = inside the house. A deadline commitment word.

sentences.kannada: Review ಮಾಡಿ ಕಳಿಸ್ತೀನಿ, ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ time ಕೊಡಿ
sentences.roman: review maadi kalistini, svalpa time kodi
sentences.gloss: Will review and send, give me some time
sentences.grammar_note: kali = send (not to be confused with kali = learn). kalisu = to send. kalistini = I will send. SMS kalistini = will send SMS. doc kalistini = will send the document.

sentences.kannada: ನಿಮ್ಮ comment ಸರಿ ಇದೆ, ಆದ್ರೆ ಒಂದ್ ಸಾರಿ ನೋಡ್ಕೊಳ್ಳಿ
sentences.roman: nimma comment sari ide, aadre ondu saari noodkolli
sentences.gloss: Your comment is right but have a look once
sentences.grammar_note: noodu = see/look. noodkolli = please look / check yourself. noodkobeku = need to look. ondu saari noodkolli = please check once. Standard code review / document review language.

sentences.kannada: ಅದು ನನ್ನ mistake, ನಾನೇ fix ಮಾಡ್ತೀನಿ
sentences.roman: adu nanna mistake, naane fix maadteeni
sentences.gloss: That is my mistake, I will fix it myself
sentences.grammar_note: naane = I myself (emphatic). naane maadteeni = I myself will do it. neevu aadte beda, naane maadteeni = you don't need to do it, I will do it myself.

sentences.kannada: Sprint-ಅಲ್ಲಿ ತುಂಬಾ story ಇದೆ, priority set ಮಾಡ್ಬೇಕು
sentences.roman: sprint-alli tumbaa story ide, priority set maadbekuu
sentences.gloss: There are a lot of stories in the sprint, need to set priority
sentences.grammar_note: Complete Bangalore tech sentence. All English nouns, Kannada grammar. maadbekuu = need to do (beku with emphatic uu). The double-u at the end is an oral emphasis marker very common in Kannada.

sentences.kannada: ನಾವು ಇಲ್ಲಿ small function ಮಾಡ್ತಾ ಇದ್ದೀವಿ, ಬನ್ನಿ
sentences.roman: naavu illi small function maadtaa iddeevi, banni
sentences.gloss: We are doing a small function here, please come
sentences.grammar_note: maadtaa iddeevi = we are doing (ongoing, plural). iddeevi = we are here. hogteevi = we are going. The we-form: -teevi/-iddeevi. An informal invitation to a home event.

sentences.kannada: ಮನೆ ಹಾಸಿಗೆ ಸಮಾರಂಭ ಇದೆ, ಬನ್ನಿ ಊಟ ಮಾಡ್ಕೊಂಡು ಹೋಗಿ
sentences.roman: mane haasige samaarambha ide, banni oota maadkondu hogi
sentences.gloss: There is a house-warming function, come eat and go
sentences.grammar_note: samaarambha = event or ceremony. haasige samaarambha = house-warming. oota maadkondu hogi = eat and go. -kondu = having done (for yourself). A warm Kannada hospitality phrase.

sentences.kannada: ಇವ್ರು ನನ್ ಕಾಲೇಜ್ ಫ್ರೆಂಡ್, ಇಲ್ಲೇ ಕೆಲ್ಸ ಮಾಡ್ತಾರೆ
sentences.roman: ivru nan college friend, ille kelsa maadtaare
sentences.gloss: This is my college friend, works right here
sentences.grammar_note: ivaru = this person (polite). ivru = this person (spoken casual). avaru = that person (polite). avru = that person (spoken casual). The -u shortens in fast speech. An informal introduction.

sentences.kannada: ಅವನ ಜೊತೆ ಮಾತಾಡ್ತೀನಿ, ಅವನು ಹೇಳ್ತಾನೆ ಅಂದ್ರೆ ಸರಿ
sentences.roman: avana jote maataadteeni, avanu heltaane amdre sari
sentences.gloss: I will talk with him, if he says it is fine then ok
sentences.grammar_note: helu = say. heltaane = he says (casual, same generation). heltaare = he says (polite). avanu heltaane amdre sari = if he says it then fine. The indirect decision-making pattern.

sentences.kannada: ಅವಳು ತುಂಬಾ busy ಇರ್ತಾಳೆ, ಮೊದ್ಲು ಕೇಳ್ಕೊಂಡು ಹೋಗಿ
sentences.roman: avalu tumbaa busy irtaale, modlu kelkondu hogi
sentences.gloss: She is usually very busy, check with her before going
sentences.new_word_meaning: she usually is / she tends to be
sentences.grammar_note: iru + taale = irtaale = she is (habitual, casual female). irtaane = he is (habitual, casual male). irtaare = they/he/she is (habitual, polite). Habitual state, not just now.

sentences.gloss: What are your weekend plans? Going for an outing?
sentences.grammar_note: outing hogteera = going for an outing. outing maadteevi = doing an outing. plan enu = what is the plan. A completely natural Bangalore Friday question.

sentences.gloss: No plan at all, will stay home itself
sentences.grammar_note: mane + le = manele = at home (emphatic, I'm not going anywhere). illi + le = illi le = right here. The -le suffix emphasizes staying in that location.

sentences.kannada: Cubbon Park-ಗೆ ಹೋಗ್ಬೇಕು, ತುಂಬಾ ದಿನ ಆಯ್ತು ಹೋಗಿ
sentences.roman: Cubbon Park-ge hogbeku, tumbaa dina aaytu hogi
sentences.gloss: Need to go to Cubbon Park, it has been many days since going
sentences.grammar_note: tumbaa dina aaytu hogi = many days have become since having gone. hogi = having gone (connector). aaytu with connector verb expresses time elapsed since an activity.

sentences.kannada: Nandi Hills early morning ಹೋಗಿ ಬಂದ್ರೆ ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿರತ್ತೆ
sentences.roman: Nandi Hills early morning hogi bandre tumbaa chennaagiratte
sentences.gloss: If you go to Nandi Hills early morning it will be very nice
sentences.new_word_meaning: will be nice / will be good
sentences.grammar_note: chennaagide = is good. chennaagiratte = will be good / would be good. bandre = if you come back. hogi bandre = if you go and come back. The Bangalore Sunday trip recommendation.

sentences.kannada: Sakleshpur trip plan ಮಾಡ್ತಾ ಇದ್ದೀವಿ, ನೀವೂ ಬರ್ತೀರಾ?
sentences.roman: Sakleshpur trip plan maadtaa iddeevi, neevoo barteera?
sentences.gloss: We are planning a Sakleshpur trip, will you also come?
sentences.grammar_note: neevu + oo = neevoo = you too / also you. naanoo = I too. avranoo = them too. The oo suffix is the and-also particle. neevoo barteera = will you also come.

sentences.gloss: I had told him, but he does not listen
sentences.new_word_meaning: I had told / I had said
sentences.grammar_note: helu = say. hellidde = I had said (past perfect, I did say). kelalla = does not listen / will not listen. adu gottilla anta hellidde = I had told him he didn't know. Expressing having done something that was ignored.

sentences.kannada: ಅವಳು ಹೇಳ್ದ್ದು ನಿಜ ಅಂತ ಈಗ ಗೊತ್ತಾಯ್ತು
sentences.roman: avalu helddu nija anta eega gottaaytu
sentences.gloss: What she said was true, I understand now
sentences.new_word_meaning: what was said / the thing said
sentences.grammar_note: helu + ddu = helddu = what was said. avalu helddu = what she said. avanu maadiddu = what he did. Nominalizing a past verb with -ddu to make it a noun-phrase.

sentences.gloss: That time I should not have gone
sentences.new_word_meaning: should not have gone (past negative obligation)
sentences.grammar_note: hogabaaradu = should not go. hogabaradittu = should not have gone. maadabaaradu = should not do. maadabaradittu = should not have done. The regret/hindsight construction.

sentences.gloss: Should have said earlier, right?
sentences.grammar_note: modalu = before/first, spoken as modle. helbekittu = should have said. bekittu = should have been. A gentle reproach that could apply to anything — plans, problems, information withheld.

sentences.gloss: That is not his fault, the situation was like that
sentences.new_word_meaning: not a mistake / not at fault
sentences.grammar_note: tappu + alla = tapalla = not a mistake. avana tapalla = not his fault. nanna tapalla = not my fault. haagittu = was like that (haage + ittu). Defending someone with the situation-was-like-that explanation.

sentences.kannada: ನಾನು ಅವ್ರ ಜಾಗದಲ್ಲಿ ಇದ್ದಿದ್ರೆ ನಾನೂ ಹಾಗೇ ಮಾಡ್ತಿದ್ದೆ
sentences.roman: naanu avra jaagadalli iddidre naanoo haage maadtidde
sentences.gloss: If I were in their place I would have done the same
sentences.grammar_note: jaaga = place or position. avra jaagadalli = in their place. iddidre = if I were. haage maadtidde = would have done the same. The empathy construction in Kannada.

sentences.kannada: ಅವ್ರಿಗೆ ಕಷ್ಟ ಆಗಿದೆ, ನಾವು ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ help ಮಾಡ್ಬೇಕು
sentences.roman: avrige kashta aagide, naavu svalpa help maadbekuu
sentences.gloss: They are having difficulty, we need to help a little
sentences.grammar_note: avrige = to them / for them (spoken shortening of avvarige). avanu + ge = avanige = to him. avalu + ge = avalige = to her. avarge = to them. Empathy followed by action.

sentences.kannada: ಏನೂ ಆಗಲ್ಲ, ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ time ಕೊಡಿ, ಸರಿ ಆಗತ್ತೆ
sentences.roman: enu aagalla, svalpa time kodi, sari aagatte
sentences.gloss: Nothing will happen, give it some time, it will be fine
sentences.grammar_note: enu aagalla = nothing will happen = it will be fine. sari aagatte = will become right. The Bangalore reassurance formula: don't worry + give time + it will work out.

sentences.gloss: What you said is right, I will think about it
sentences.new_word_meaning: I will think / I will consider
sentences.grammar_note: yochisu = to think / consider. yochissteeni = I will think about it. yochisi maadu = think and do. The polite non-committal response — I hear you, I will consider it.

sentences.gloss: Let us talk about this one more time
sentences.grammar_note: bagge = about / regarding. idara bagge = about this. nimma bagge = about you. work bagge = about the work. idu bagge = about this. The natural way to say let us discuss this further.

sentences.kannada: ನಿನ್ನೆ ಮಳೆ ತುಂಬಾ ಬಿದ್ದಿತ್ತು, ರಸ್ತೆ ಎಲ್ಲಾ flood ಆಗಿತ್ತು
sentences.roman: ninne male tumbaa bidditu, raste ellaa flood aagittu
sentences.gloss: Yesterday rain fell heavily, all the roads were flooded
sentences.grammar_note: bidu = to fall. bidditu = it fell (past). male bidditu = rain fell. Bengalooru-alli male bidditu = it rained in Bangalore. flood aagittu = was flooded (past of aagide).

sentences.gloss: In the rainy season Bengaluru traffic is even more difficult
sentences.grammar_note: male = rain. gaala = season/time. malegaala = rainy season. beligaala = summer. chali gaala = winter/cool season. malegaaladalli = during the rainy season.

sentences.kannada: Power cut ಆಗಿದೆ, UPS-ಅಲ್ಲಿ ಕೆಲಸ ಮಾಡ್ತಿದ್ದೀನಿ
sentences.roman: power cut aagide, UPS-alli kelasa maadtiddeeni
sentences.gloss: Power cut has happened, working on UPS
sentences.grammar_note: power cut aagide = power cut has happened. maadtiddeeni = I am working (continuous). UPS-alli = on UPS power. An extremely Bangalore sentence — power cuts are a shared experience.

sentences.kannada: Water ಸಮಸ್ಯೆ ಇದೆ ಈ area-ಅಲ್ಲಿ, daily ಒಂದೇ ಗಂಟೆ ಬರತ್ತೆ
sentences.roman: water samasye ide ee area-alli, daily omde gamte baratte
sentences.gloss: There is a water problem in this area, it comes only one hour daily
sentences.grammar_note: samasye = problem. samasyе ide = there is a problem. samasye aagide = problem has occurred. ee area-alli water samasye ide = water problem in this area. omde = only one (emphatic).

sentences.kannada: Rent ಪ್ರತಿ ವರ್ಷ ಹೆಚ್ಚಾಗ್ತಿದೆ, shift ಮಾಡ್ಬೇಕು ಅನ್ಸ್ತಿದೆ
sentences.roman: rent prati varsha hecchaagide, shift maadbekuu ansttide
sentences.gloss: Rent increases every year, thinking of shifting
sentences.new_word_meaning: it feels like / I am thinking
sentences.grammar_note: annistu = to feel / to seem. ansttide = it seems / it feels. shift maadbekuu ansttide = feels like need to shift. hogbeku ansttide = feels like should go. The tentative inner thought expression.

sentences.kannada: ಈ locality ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿದೆ, connectivity ಸಹ ಒಳ್ಳೆದಿದೆ
sentences.roman: ee locality tumbaa chennaagide, connectivity saha olledigee
sentences.gloss: This locality is very nice, connectivity is also good
sentences.grammar_note: saha = also / too. connectivity saha = connectivity also. neevu saha = you also. Used like 'too' at the end. Different from kuda which also means also but saha is more formal.

sentences.gloss: 2BHK is available for 25,000, not bad right?
sentences.new_word_meaning: not bad / no objection / it is fine
sentences.grammar_note: paravaagilla = no problem / not bad / acceptable. paravaagilla ide = it is passable. paravaagilla alvaa = not bad right? The Kannada equivalent of 'it's decent'.

sentences.kannada: ಇಲ್ಲಿ parking swalpa problem, ಅದೊಂದು ಬಿಟ್ರೆ ಮತ್ತೆಲ್ಲ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿದೆ
sentences.roman: illi parking svalpa problem, adombdu bitre mattella chennaagide
sentences.gloss: Parking is a little problem here, other than that everything is good
sentences.new_word_meaning: other than / except (if you leave aside)
sentences.grammar_note: bidu = to leave. bitre = if you leave aside / except. adombdu bitre = except for that one thing. idu bitre ellaa sari = except this, everything is fine.

sentences.gloss: School is nearby itself, convenient for children
sentences.grammar_note: anukoola = convenient or suitable. anukoola ide = is convenient. makkаlige anukoola = convenient for the children. hatrane = near itself (hatra + ne, the emphatic near).

sentences.gloss: Let us see first, will decide after
sentences.new_word_meaning: let us see / let us look
sentences.grammar_note: noodu = see. nodona = let us see. nodondre = if you see. modlu nodona, aamele decide maadteevi = let us look first, then decide. The classic Bangalore property viewing sentence.

sentences.gloss: Why does that always happen to me specifically
sentences.grammar_note: yaavaaga = when. yaavaagloo = always (yaavaaga + oo = every time). yaavaagloo late aagatte = always gets late. nange aagatte = happens to me. The universal complaint about bad luck.

sentences.kannada: Scene innu clear aaglilla, wait maadona
sentences.roman: scene innu clear aaglilla, wait maadona
sentences.gloss: The situation is not clear yet, let us wait
sentences.new_word_meaning: has not become / did not happen yet
sentences.grammar_note: aaglilla = has not become / did not happen. clear aaglilla = has not cleared. aaytu = happened. aaglilla = did not happen. innu aaglilla = still has not happened.

sentences.kannada: Full confusion aagide, yaaru enu heltaaro gottilla
sentences.roman: full confusion aagide, yaaru enu heltaaro gottilla
sentences.gloss: Total confusion, do not know who is saying what
sentences.grammar_note: heltaaro = who is saying (embedded question). enu maadtaaro gottilla = do not know what they are doing. The -aaro suffix embeds a question as indirect speech.

sentences.gloss: Should have listened to what they said
sentences.grammar_note: maatu = spoken word or speech. avru helddu maatu = the words they said. maatu kaelu = to listen to words = to heed advice. maatu ketilla = did not listen = did not follow advice.

sentences.gloss: What to do now, it has already happened
sentences.new_word_meaning: it went / it already happened (cannot be undone)
sentences.grammar_note: hogу + tu = hoytu = it went = it happened (and is now gone/done). aagi hoytu = happened and went. Cannot be undone. The acceptance phrase after a mistake or misfortune.

sentences.gloss: Even so it is fine, got the experience
sentences.grammar_note: sigu = get/find/meet. sikkitu = got (past). experience sikkitu = got experience. friend sikkitu = found a friend. sigu covers getting, finding, and meeting.

sentences.gloss: Such a good opportunity will not come again
sentences.grammar_note: avakasha = opportunity. olle avakasha = good opportunity. avakasha sikkitu = got an opportunity. avakasha bidabekilla = should not miss the opportunity.

sentences.kannada: ಅವನು ತುಂಬಾ ಮುಂದೆ ಹೋಗ್ತಾನೆ, ಆ ಮನೆ ಇದೆ ಅವನಿಗೆ
sentences.roman: avanu tumbaa munde hogtaane, aa mane ide avanige
sentences.gloss: He will go far, he has that quality in him
sentences.new_word_meaning: will go forward / will progress / will succeed
sentences.grammar_note: munde hogu = to go forward = to progress/succeed. munde hogteeni = I am progressing. avanu tumbaa munde hogtaane = he will go very far. A compliment about someone's potential.

sentences.gloss: She will not give up, she is strong
sentences.new_word_meaning: will not give up / will not let go
sentences.grammar_note: bidu = to leave/let go. bittu kodu = to give up. bittu kodalla = will not give up. strong aagiddaale = she has become strong (sustained state, female). Talking about someone's resilience.

sentences.kannada: ಈ city-ಅಲ್ಲಿ survive ಮಾಡ್ಬೇಕಂದ್ರೆ ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ tough ಆಗ್ಬೇಕು
sentences.roman: ee city-alli survive maadbekandre svalpa tough aagbeku
sentences.gloss: To survive in this city you need to be a little tough
sentences.new_word_meaning: if you want to / in order to
sentences.grammar_note: beku + andre = bekandre = in order to / if you want to. survive maadbekandre = if you want to survive. succeed aagbekandre = in order to succeed. The purpose/condition construction.

sentences.kannada: ಅವರ ಮನೆಯಲ್ಲಿ ಊಟ ಮಾಡಿದ್ದೆ, ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿತ್ತು
sentences.roman: avara maneyalli oota maadidde, tumbaa chennaagittu
sentences.gloss: Had eaten at their home, it was very good
sentences.new_word_meaning: I had done / I did (past, first person)
sentences.grammar_note: maadidde = I did / I had done. hellidde = I had said. noodidde = I had seen. The -idde ending is past tense, first person. chennaagittu = was good (past of chennaagide).

sentences.gloss: I feel like going there again
sentences.new_word_meaning: it feels / it seems / there is a feeling
sentences.grammar_note: annisu = to feel/seem. annisttide = it is feeling. hogbeku anta annisttide = there is a feeling of wanting to go. A softer version of I want to — expresses desire as a sensation.

sentences.kannada: ಆ restaurant ಮುಚ್ಚಿ ಹೋಯ್ತು, ತುಂಬಾ miss ಆಗ್ತಿದೆ
sentences.roman: aa restaurant mucchi hoytu, tumbaa miss aagide
sentences.gloss: That restaurant closed down, missing it a lot
sentences.grammar_note: muchu = to close. mucchi = having closed. mucchi hoytu = closed and went away = shut down permanently. miss aagide = is being missed. Lamenting a closed favourite is a Bangalore conversation staple.

sentences.kannada: ಮೊದಲು Bengalooru ತುಂಬಾ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿತ್ತು, pension ಪಡೆಯೋರ ಊರು ಅಂತ ಇತ್ತು
sentences.roman: modalu Bengalooru tumbaa chennaagittu, pension padeyora ooru anta ittu
sentences.gloss: Earlier Bengaluru was very nice, it was known as the city of retirees
sentences.grammar_note: padeyu = to receive/get. padeyoru = those who get. pension padeyoru = pensioners. The old description of Bangalore as a retirement city before the tech boom.

sentences.kannada: Garden City ಅಂತ ಇತ್ತು, ಈಗ Concrete Jungle ಆಗಿದೆ
sentences.roman: Garden City anta ittu, eega Concrete Jungle aagide
sentences.gloss: It used to be called Garden City, now it has become a Concrete Jungle
sentences.grammar_note: anta ittu = used to be called. anta ide = is called / is said to be. aagide = has become. The classic Bangalore nostalgia sentence, heard in any conversation about the city's transformation.

sentences.gloss: Even so I will stay here, not going anywhere
sentences.grammar_note: elli = where. elloo = everywhere / anywhere. elloo hogalla = not going anywhere. elloo sigalla = not found anywhere. With a negative verb, elloo = nowhere.

sentences.gloss: This city has somehow held on to me
sentences.new_word_meaning: has caught / has held / has grabbed
sentences.grammar_note: hiddu = to catch/hold. hidkonide = has held on. nannanna = me (object, accusative). city nannanna hidkonide = the city has caught me. The magnetic feeling Bangalore creates — you complain but cannot leave.

sentences.kannada: Namma Bengalooru, adu adu thara iratte
sentences.roman: namma Bengalooru, adu adu thara iratte
sentences.gloss: Our Bengaluru, it will be the way it is
sentences.new_word_meaning: the way it is / in its own way
sentences.grammar_note: adu adu thara = the way it is / its own way. adu adu thara iratte = it will be the way it is. Accepting Bangalore's chaos with affection. The philosophical shrug of a Bangalorean.

sentences.kannada: ಒಂದ್ ದಿನ ಇದೆಲ್ಲ ಸರಿ ಹೋಗತ್ತೆ ಅಂತ ನಂಬ್ಕೊಂಡಿದ್ದೀನಿ
sentences.roman: ondu dina idella sari hogatte anta nambkoniddini
sentences.gloss: I believe that one day all of this will be fine
sentences.new_word_meaning: I believe / I have kept faith
sentences.grammar_note: nambu = to believe/trust. nambkoniddini = I believe (and am holding onto that belief). idella = all this. sari hogatte = will become right. The optimist's Bangalore sentence.

sentences.kannada: Swalpa swalpa change aagatte, time kottu nodona
sentences.roman: svalpa svalpa change aagatte, time kottu nodona
sentences.gloss: Change will come little by little, give time and let us see
sentences.grammar_note: kodu = give. kottu = having given (connector). time kottu nodona = giving time, let us see. The patient Bangalore optimism about city improvement — and life in general.

sentences.gloss: Will send on WhatsApp, read it later
sentences.grammar_note: odu = to read. odi = read (polite command). odi nodi = read and see (check it). WhatsApp-alli kalistini = will send on WhatsApp. The Bangalore instruction for any document or information.

sentences.gloss: Shall I talk on call or shall I message?
sentences.grammar_note: maadu + laa = maadlaa = shall I do. helilaa = shall I say. hogulaa = shall I go. The -laa ending is shall-I, an offer or request for permission. call-alli maataadlaa = shall I call you?

sentences.kannada: ನಾನು ಈಗ busy, ಒಂದ್ ಗಂಟೆ ನಂತರ ಮಾತಾಡ್ಬಹುದಾ?
sentences.roman: naanu eega busy, omdu gamte namtara maataadabohudaa?
sentences.gloss: I am busy now, can we talk after one hour?
sentences.grammar_note: maataadabohuduaa = can we talk. The -abohuduaa converts any verb to can we. hogabohuduaa = can we go. nodabohuduaa = can we see/meet. A full question with embedded deferral.

sentences.kannada: Location share ಮಾಡ್ತೀನಿ, ನೀವು track ಮಾಡ್ಕೊಳ್ಳಿ
sentences.roman: location share maadteeni, neevu track maadkolli
sentences.gloss: I will share location, you keep track
sentences.grammar_note: share maadteeni = I will share. track maadkolli = you track (for yourself). Coordinating meetups in Bangalore always involves this exchange. maadkolli = please do it for your own benefit.

sentences.kannada: Battery 10% ಇದೆ, charge ಆಗ್ತಿದೆ, ಸ್ವಲ್ಪ wait
sentences.roman: battery 10% ide, charge aagtide, svalpa wait
sentences.gloss: Battery is at 10%, it is charging, wait a little
sentences.grammar_note: charge aagtide = is charging (happening now). aaytu = has charged. aagilla = has not charged. The phone battery sentence — universally understood across all Bangalore conversations.

sentences.kannada: ನೆಟ್ ಸ್ಲೋ ಆಗಿದೆ, video call ಕಷ್ಟ ಆಗ್ತಿದೆ
sentences.roman: net slow aagide, video call kashta aagtide
sentences.gloss: Net is slow, video call is becoming difficult
sentences.grammar_note: net slow aagide = net has become slow. kashta aagtide = is becoming difficult. Two English adjectives, Kannada grammar — the code-switch is seamless and completely natural.

sentences.gloss: Do a reboot, it usually gets fixed
sentences.grammar_note: saamaanya = normal/common. saamaanyavaagi = normally/usually. saamaanyavaagi aagatte = usually it works out. saamaanyavaagi late aagalla = usually it doesn't get late. The qualifier for typical behavior.

sentences.kannada: ಇದು install ಆಗ್ತಿಲ್ಲ, ಬೇರೆ version try ಮಾಡು
sentences.roman: idu install aagtilla, bere version try maadu
sentences.gloss: This is not installing, try a different version
sentences.grammar_note: install aagtilla = is not installing. bere version = different version. try maadu = try doing it. Complete tech troubleshooting sentence in Bangalore Kannada — all English nouns, Kannada grammar and flow.

sentences.kannada: ನಿಮ್ಮ ಸಹಾಯ ಇಲ್ಲದೇ ಈ project ಮುಗಿಯೋದು ಕಷ್ಟ ಆಗ್ತಿತ್ತು
sentences.roman: nimma sahaaya illade ee project mugiyodu kashta aagtittu
sentences.gloss: Without your help this project would have been difficult to finish
sentences.grammar_note: mugi = to finish/end. mugiyodu = the finishing (nominalized). illade = without. aagtittu = would have become (past conditional). A complete gratitude sentence expressing dependency.

sentences.gloss: If we do it together everything will happen
sentences.grammar_note: naavu ottige = we together. maadidre = if we do. ella aagatte = everything will happen. The simplest, most complete expression of the Bangalore collaborative spirit. A fitting final sentence.
